content: Curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods (ICM/RND) and 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory share an underrecognized underlying principle: both prioritize "reserving memory capacity for high-uncertainty states" rather than storing all encountered experience equally. ICM/RND intrinsically reward exploration of uncertain states, while SOTA active memory dynamically allocates storage based on information gain, creating a natural cross-pollination opportunity to embed intrinsic curiosity signals directly into memory allocation gates.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
